value.found_quote: Guardrails validate input, output, or tool behavior automatically... Block disallowed user requests before the main model runs [Input guardrails] | Validate or redact the final output before it leaves the system [Output guardrails] | Check arguments or results around a function tool call [Tool guardrails]
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes OpenAI's guardrail system for agents, covering input guardrails, output guardrails, and tool guardrails. It confirms that guardrails automatically validate, sanitize/redact, and block content around agent runs — covering input (blocking disallowed requests), output (validating or redacting final output), and tool calls (checking arguments/results). Code examples show programmatic guardrail definitions. The page also describes human-in-the-loop approvals as a complementary mechanism. All aspects of the claim are substantiated by the page content, and the feature appears to be generally available (active), with no indication of preview or deprecation status.
